The Agent Trust Stack.
Skyfire gives agents
verified identity and payment credentials
that work across the open
          internet. No 403 errors, captchas or blocked checkouts. Authenticated access and payment capability,
          everywhere your agents need to be.

What KYA Powers
One token, two surfaces.
An extensible identity token accepted by the majority of the commercial internet, enabling the entire agentic
          customer journey from access to login to checkout.
KYA · Access
Access & Login
A portable, verified identity token accepted by the majority of the commercial internet. KYA is designed to
            give your agent programmatic access and log-in capabilities to websites, applications and agent protocols
            with little engineering effort.

Agentic Commerce
Checkout & Payments
A complete agentic commerce wallet that allows your agents to securely complete checkouts via websites and
            emerging agentic protocols. Skyfire partners will all major credit card networks to allow tokenized card
            transactions and user mandate collection so agents can reliably checkout out at the scale of the internet.

Product Stack
One unified trust stack.
01
Know Your
Agent
Give your agents controlled access with a standardized open identity protocol designed to
            unblock websites, services and checkouts.
Learn about KYA
→
02
Agentic
Wallet
Give your agents a secure wallet able to enroll user credit cards, manage stablecoins and
            collect user mandates.
See Use Cases
→
03
Buy
for Me
A complete agentic purchase experience on behalf of users with secure transactions
            leveraging existing referal links for checkout.
See Use Cases
→
Access
Enable Agent Access
at Internet Scale
Teams that operate agents need reliable access to websites and applications. KYA tokens from Skyfire bind
            platform, agent, and human principal identities along with user mandates to allow seamless access at scale,
            across the open web.

Powered by
Developer Documentation
Identity and Payments for AI. This guide provides technical instructions for using the Skyfire platform as both a Buyer and Seller.
Overview
Skyfire enables programmatic interaction between
Buyers
and
Sellers
using a token-based identity and payment protocol.
Each user manages one or more agent accounts (Buyer or Seller).  Buyers interact with seller websites, APIs, MCP servers, and services by presenting Skyfire tokens:
kya
(identity)
pay
(payment)
kya-pay
(combined identity and payment)
Platform Structure
Users
A
User
is the top-level entity on the Skyfire platform. Users typically represent an individual person or business.
Users:
Manage one or more
agent accounts
Maintain a verified identity
Control API keys and wallet access
A user's verified identity may be leveraged by their agents for:
Account creation
Authentication
Access to seller services
Agent Accounts
Agent accounts are the operational entities that interact with Skyfire APIs.
Each agent:
Is tied to exactly one user
Has exactly one role:
Buyer
or
Seller
Is accessed programmatically via unique API keys.
Each user may have:
One
Buyer agent account
(created by default at signup).
One optional
Seller agent account
(created via the Skyfire Dashboard)
Each agent may have one or more API keys for secure access.
Buyer Agent
Acts on behalf of the user to discover and purchase services or tools from sellers.
Capabilities:
Can create accounts with seller services
Subscribe to or purchase services
Generate:
kya
tokens (identity)
pay
tokens (payment)
kya-pay
tokens (identity + payment)
Each buyer agent has a
wallet
, funded by the user, used to transact with sellers.
When calling a seller service, the buyer includes the appropriate token as specified by the seller (e.g. HTTP header or request body).
Seller Agent
Enables a user to publish and monetize services, tools, or APIs.
Seller agents:
Must be explicitly created via the Skyfire Dashboard
Register
Seller Services
Define identity and payment requirements for buyer requests
Verify incoming tokens via standard JWKS libraries to:
Authenticate the buyer (
kya
)
Charge the buyer (
pay
or
kya-pay
)
Sellers receive payments via Skyfire's settlement system once services are delivered.
Wallets
Each agent account comes with an associated wallet, managed by Skyfire.
Buyers
fund their wallets to purchase services and tools
Sellers
receive funds in their wallets after successfully charging buyer tokens and delivering the service
Skyfire handles
settlement and transfer
of funds between wallets after a successful transaction.
Seller Services
Seller services are the products, tools, or APIs made available by seller agents to buyers.
A seller can define
multiple seller services
under one seller agent.
Services may include:
Remote/local MCP servers
APIs
Websites
Each Seller Service specifies:
Pricing
Identity requirements
Accepted token types
Token format and expected delivery method (e.g.
skyfire-pay-id
header)
Identity Requirements
Seller Services may optionally enforce identity requirements.
If
no identity requirements
are specified:
Any buyer can create tokens for it
The buyer principal's email address will be included in the
hid
claim
If identity requirements are specified:
Only buyers that satisfy those identity requirements can create tokens for it
Rules
:
If only
business identity
is required:
Only buyers that have verified their business identity can create tokens
Buyers that have NOT verified identity OR have verified individual identity CANNOT create tokens
If only
individual identity
is required
Only buyers that have verified their individual identity can create tokens
Buyers that have NOT verified identity OR have verified business identity CANNOT create tokens
If both
individual and business identity
is required
Only buyers that have verified either their individual identity or their business identity can create tokens
Buyers that have NOT verified identity at all CANNOT create tokens
Buyers with no verified identity cannot create tokens for restricted services
Services can be:
Paid
, require
pay
or
kya-pay
tokens
Free
, require
kya
tokens (identity only)
Updated
3 months ago
What’s Next
Understanding KYA+Pay Tokens
KYA+Pay Tokens

Yes
No

Skyfire lets AI agents spend your money

analysis.name: Skyfire
analysis.value_proposition: Skyfire gives AI agents verified identity tokens and payment credentials to autonomously access websites, authenticate, and complete purchases across the open internet.
analysis.ideal_customer: Teams building AI agents that need to browse, log in, and transact on the web on behalf of users

features.name: kya token (know your agent identity token)
features.description: Standardized open identity token binding platform, agent, and human principal identities for programmatic web access
features.evidence_quote: kya (identity) — a portable, verified identity token accepted by the majority of the commercial internet

features.name: pay token
features.description: Payment-only token allowing agents to authorize charges on behalf of a user
features.evidence_quote: pay (payment) — Each buyer agent has a wallet, funded by the user, used to transact with sellers.

features.name: kya-pay token (combined identity and payment)
features.description: Single token carrying both verified identity and payment authorization for agentic transactions
features.evidence_quote: kya-pay (combined identity and payment) — Generate: kya tokens (identity), pay tokens (payment), kya-pay tokens (identity + payment)

features.name: agent wallet
features.description: Secure wallet per agent account for holding funds, enrolling user credit cards, and managing stablecoins
features.evidence_quote: Give your agents a secure wallet able to enroll user credit cards, manage stablecoins and collect user mandates.

features.name: buy for me
features.description: End-to-end agentic purchase flow on behalf of users using existing merchant referral links
features.evidence_quote: A complete agentic purchase experience on behalf of users with secure transactions leveraging existing referal links for checkout.

features.name: agent access & login
features.description: Programmatic site access and authenticated login linking agent and human identity via KYA token
features.evidence_quote: Give your agent programmatic access and log-in capabilities to websites, applications and agent protocols with little engineering effort.

features.name: agentic checkout & payments
features.description: Agents complete web checkouts via tokenized card transactions across major card networks
features.evidence_quote: A complete agentic commerce wallet that allows your agents to securely complete checkouts via websites and emerging agentic protocols.

features.name: user mandate collection
features.description: Collects and stores user-granted spending mandates (merchant, product, amount) authorizing agent transactions
features.evidence_quote: User mandate for merchant, product, and amount. Agentic Wallet authorizes tokenized card for payment.

features.name: tokenized card transactions
features.description: Credit card tokenization via Visa, Mastercard, Discover networks so agents checkout without exposing raw card data
features.evidence_quote: Skyfire partners will all major credit card networks to allow tokenized card transactions and user mandate collection

features.name: stablecoin wallet (usdc)
features.description: Agents can hold and transact with USDC stablecoins in addition to fiat credit cards
features.evidence_quote: USDC [logo shown alongside Visa, Mastercard, Discover as accepted payment methods]

features.name: buyer agent accounts
features.description: Operational agent entity that discovers, subscribes to, and purchases seller services on behalf of a user
features.evidence_quote: Buyer Agent — Acts on behalf of the user to discover and purchase services or tools from sellers.

features.name: seller agent accounts
features.description: Operational entity enabling a user to publish, monetize, and gate services/APIs with identity and payment requirements
features.evidence_quote: Seller Agent — Enables a user to publish and monetize services, tools, or APIs.

features.name: api key management
features.description: Per-agent API keys for programmatic platform access; each agent may hold one or more keys
features.evidence_quote: Each agent may have one or more API keys for secure access.

features.name: wallet funding
features.description: User-controlled funding of buyer agent wallets to enable downstream transactions
features.evidence_quote: Buyers fund their wallets to purchase services and tools

features.name: settlement system
features.description: Skyfire handles fund settlement and transfer between buyer and seller wallets after successful transactions
features.evidence_quote: Skyfire handles settlement and transfer of funds between wallets after a successful transaction.

features.name: seller services registry
features.description: Sellers define multiple services (MCP servers, APIs, websites) with pricing and identity requirements under one agent
features.evidence_quote: A seller can define multiple seller services under one seller agent. Services may include: Remote/local MCP servers, APIs, Websites

features.name: skyfire directory
features.description: Discoverable catalog of registered seller services for buyers to find and purchase
features.evidence_quote: Skyfire Directory — Service Discovery, Verified Service Guarantee

features.name: service discovery
features.description: Buyers can programmatically discover available seller services through the Skyfire platform
features.evidence_quote: Service Discovery [listed under Skyfire Directory navigation]

features.name: verified service guarantee
features.description: Platform-level guarantee attesting that listed seller services meet Skyfire's verification standards
features.evidence_quote: Verified Service Guarantee [listed under Skyfire Directory]

features.name: identity verification (individual)
features.description: Buyers can verify individual identity; seller services may require it to gate access
features.evidence_quote: If only individual identity is required: Only buyers that have verified their individual identity can create tokens

features.name: identity verification (business)
features.description: Buyers can verify business identity; seller services may require it to restrict access to business accounts only
features.evidence_quote: If only business identity is required: Only buyers that have verified their business identity can create tokens

features.name: jwks token verification
features.description: Sellers verify incoming KYA/pay tokens using standard JWKS libraries — no proprietary SDK required
features.evidence_quote: Verify incoming tokens via standard JWKS libraries to: Authenticate the buyer (kya), Charge the buyer (pay or kya-pay)

features.name: protocol support (mcp / ucp / a2a)
features.description: KYA tokens work across emerging agent protocols including MCP, UCP, and A2A
features.evidence_quote: Agent Protocols — MCP, UCP, A2A…

features.name: captcha and 403 bypass for agents
features.description: KYA token signals trusted agent identity to bot-detection and security layers, eliminating access blocks
features.evidence_quote: No 403 errors, captchas or blocked checkouts. Authenticated access and payment capability, everywhere your agents need to be.

features.name: bot manager / security layer integration
features.description: Pre-integrated with bot management vendors so KYA tokens are natively trusted by security infrastructure
features.evidence_quote: Powering agentic commerce through leading infrastructure partners — f5-logo-black, HUMAN Security, Enable Agent Acceptance →

features.name: buyer onboarding flow
features.description: Guided account creation and setup workflow for buyer agents including identity verification and wallet funding
features.evidence_quote: Buyer Workflows — Buyer Account Creation, Buyer Onboarding, Know Your Agent (KYA), Wallet Funding

features.name: seller onboarding flow
features.description: Guided account creation and setup workflow for sellers to register services and configure payment/identity requirements
features.evidence_quote: Seller Workflows — Seller Account Creation, Seller Onboarding, Know Your Agent (KYA), Skyfire Directory

features.name: token claims (hid, principal binding)
features.description: Tokens carry structured claims including human identity (hid/email), agent account, and platform binding
features.evidence_quote: the buyer principal's email address will be included in the hid claim

features.name: llms.txt / openapi index
features.description: Machine-readable docs index at /llms.txt with all pages in markdown and endpoints in OpenAPI for agent-native consumption
features.evidence_quote: For AI agents: visit https://docs.skyfire.xyz/llms.txt for an index of all pages formatted in Markdown and endpoints in OpenAPI.

features.name: skyfire dashboard
features.description: Web dashboard for managing agent accounts, API keys, and creating seller agents
features.evidence_quote: One optional Seller agent account (created via the Skyfire Dashboard)

differentiators: Covering more than 60% of the Web — KYA token accepted across the majority of the commercial internet
One token, two surfaces — single extensible identity token that handles both access/login and payment in one credential
No engineering lift from merchants — live transactions complete same as today without merchant-side changes
Open identity protocol — KYA is positioned as a standardized open protocol, not a proprietary SDK
Identity bound to agent + human + platform in a single token — not just API auth, but full principal chain
Native bot/security layer support — pre-integrated with f5, HUMAN Security, DataDome, Imperva so agents aren't flagged

pricing.notes: Pricing page returns 404. No public pricing visible. CTA on landing is 'Launch Skyfire' and 'Contact us' — likely early-stage contact-sales or invite-only. No tier structure, seat pricing, or usage caps publicly disclosed.
